title: Add Purchase Users Button with Stripe Integration
story: As a product administrator, I want a 'Purchase Users' button that opens Stripe checkout with my team and user identifiers pre-filled as the client_reference_id, so that additional license purchases are automatically attributed to my account.
description:
## Context

The existing 'Request More Users' button triggers a Zapier request. This needs to be replaced with a direct Stripe purchase flow that identifies the purchasing account.

## Behavior

Replace the Zapier-based user request flow with a 'Purchase Users' button. The button constructs a Stripe checkout URL by concatenating the team ID and user ID (delimited by underscore) as the `client_reference_id` parameter. This allows the downstream automation (Zapier -> API endpoint from MBT-454) to identify which account and user initiated the purchase.

The page should also display a 'Powered by Stripe' badge below the button, sized similarly to how Stripe displays it on their checkout page.

## Purchase Flow

1. User clicks 'Purchase Users' button
2. Browser opens Stripe checkout URL with `client_reference_id=teamId_userId`
3. User completes purchase on Stripe
4. Stripe sends transaction to Zapier
5. Zapier calls the Add Users API (MBT-454) to increase the team's license count
6. Salesforce subscription record is also updated
acceptance_criteria: A 'Purchase Users' button replaces the previous 'Request Users' Zapier-based flow
Clicking the button opens Stripe checkout in a new window
The Stripe URL includes client_reference_id with format teamId_userId
Both the account (team) and user public-facing IDs are used, not internal integer IDs
A 'Powered by Stripe' badge is displayed below the button
The old Zapier request flow is deprecated
ux_changes: Replaces the 'Request More Users' button with a 'Purchase Users' button and adds a 'Powered by Stripe' badge. Button opens Stripe checkout in a new window.
middleware_changes: Constructs the Stripe checkout URL dynamically by concatenating the team's public-facing unique ID and user's unique ID as the client_reference_id parameter.
api_changes: Integrates with Stripe checkout via URL parameter passing. Downstream Zapier automation calls POST /api/addusers with the team_id extracted from the client_reference_id.
implementation_notes: The client_reference_id format evolved during implementation. Initially used internal team_id integers, then switched to public-facing unique IDs (e.g., 74FA03E64532_74FA03E64551) to avoid exposing internal database IDs externally. The Stripe payment link format: https://buy.stripe.com/aEU2bI6ST6s7bEQeUV?client_reference_id=[teamId_userId].
comment_summary: Developer implemented the button and asked about handling the Stripe payment response to update the user count. Discussion clarified the workflow: Stripe -> Zapier -> API endpoint, using public-facing IDs rather than internal integers for security. Both team and user IDs are included in the client_reference_id.
